Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given an event log with three cases Let us analyze the traces Case 1 RA VF AE RMI VF AD SC AC Case 2 RA VF AE AD SC AC Case 3 RA VF AE RMI RMI VF AD SC AC Observations All cases start with RA then VF then AE After AE there is an optional part sometimes case 1 and 3 we have one or more RMI followed by VF and sometimes case 2 we go directly to AD After the optional part all cases have AD then SC then AC

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We need to model the process with a process tree The main sequence is RA VF AE optional loop AD SC AC

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: It seems that if there is at least one RMI then there is a VF after it The VF after AE might be a re validation of the form after missing information is provided

Name: Validation - IND
Text: In case 1 we have one RMI and then VF In case 3 two RMI and then VF which also fits In case 2 zero RMI and no VF after AE

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: But wait in case 3 we have two consecutive RMI activities That suggests that the loop might allow multiple RMI without an intermediate VF So the pattern is not simply repeated RMI VF

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We can model this as an XOR between no RMI and no VF and one or more RMI followed by VF One or more RMI can be modeled as a loop star RMI tau Then sequence that with VF So the optional part is either tau or sequence of loop RMI tau and VF

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the process tree for the optional part is X tau sequence loop RMI tau VF The root is a sequence of RA VF AE optional part AD SC AC

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Let us check if this tree produces the desired traces Case 2 zero RMI gives RA VF AE AD SC AC Case 1 one RMI gives RA VF AE RMI VF AD SC AC Case 3 two RMI gives RA VF AE RMI RMI VF AD SC AC So this tree captures the behavior
